observations.recommendation:
1.12. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) cause the timely submission of the
Bank Reconciliation Statement and other supporting schedules within the
prescribed period, in reference to COA Circular COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996. To aid in the timely verification of accounts,
checking reconciling items if valid, properly classified, and if applicable,
were correctly adjusted; and (b) facilitate the preparation of General
Ledger and Subsidiary Ledger to be used in monitoring the LGU’s
accounts; and
(b) consider the hiring of additional personnel in the Accounting Department
to help in facilitating the preparation of General Ledger and Subsidiary
Ledger in consonance with the requisite prescribed under the MNGAS.

3.42. We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and supplement additions
thereto and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) investigate noted
misstatements caused by failure to derecognize semi-expendable
properties, and erroneous recording of capitalizable expenses; (ii)
prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries, if warranted, to
correct the misstatements of the affected accounts; and (iii) henceforth,
ensure that costs of major repairs, rehabilitation, improvement, and
construction of infrastructure assets are recognized as PPE, in
accordance with IPSAS No. 45, and strictly observe the capitalization
threshold provided under COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14,
2024; and (iv) work coordinately with the Municipal Engineer on the
inventory of all local road projects with a complete description and cost
segregation of road components to ensure proper recording and control
of the assets; and (v) in case of disposal of properties, ensure that
4 6
documentary requirements are complied with prior the dropping of
properties in the accounting books; (vi) prepare the respective PPE
Ledger Cards not only for the usual accounts but also for Local Road
Network as required by the Manual on NGAS for LGUs and COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015;
(b) enjoin the Inventory Committee and other concerned officials to (i)
expedite the preparation of the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) as
required by the Circular and, if warranted, appoint additional IC
members to fast-track the cleansing of PPE records; and (ii) henceforth,
conduct periodic inventory as required by the MNGAS to avoid the
recurrence of discrepancies in the PPE records of the LGU;
(c) instruct the Disposal Committee to (i) submit the lacking documents
relative to the disposed properties; and (ii) henceforward, strictly
observe the process of disposal of unserviceable properties provided
under the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties.
(d) require the designated General Services Office to exert efforts to
prepare the Property Cards, Local Road Inventory and Road Map and
Local Road Network Card, starting with the current year’s acquisition
and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage
until all PPE items are covered.

4.25. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) set-up RPT and SET receivables and deferred
property tax at the beginning of the year based on duly certified list of the
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and year
thereafter provided by the Municipal Treasurer parallel with the
requirement of the MNGAS; (ii) recognize appropriate discounts and
penalties based on Abstract of Real Property Tax cognizant with
International standards of accounting provided above; and (iii) henceforth
observe the prescribed accounting procedures in recording RPT and SET
receivable and deferred income accounts; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) in coordination with the Municipal Assessor,
submit to the Municipal Accountant an updated and complete duly
59
certified list of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the
year and years thereafter as reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables
and deferred accounts at the beginning of the year by setting a target of a
reasonable percentage annually until all properties and RPT taxpayers are
covered; (ii) monitor and intensify collection of the delinquent real
property taxes (RPT) by implementing effective strategies such as the
request the assistance of the barangays where real property units is
situated for the immediate release of notices to concerned taxpayers; and
(iii) continue with the collection strategy being employed as at the moment
and stringent information dissemination especially at the time of grant of
tax amnesty. Otherwise, after the lapse of the amnesty recommend
imposition of civil remedies, if warranted as provided under Section 174 of
the LGC to encourage the taxpayers to pay their tax on time

5.10. We reiterated our prior year's audit recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive to:
(a) examine the noted vehicle identified in item 1.4. of this Memorandum,
submit explanation or valid justification on the use thereof, present
documentation, if warranted. Otherwise, require the refund from
personnel concerned of the amounts corresponding to the value of gasoline
issued for use of unserviceable vehicles;
(b) require the concerned drivers to prepare the trip ticket judiciously prior
to trips, leading to the completion of data needed in the trip tickets;
(c) instruct the GSO to henceforth, monitor the vehicles stated in driver’s trip
ticket to prevent the issuance of a PO pass to unserviceable motor vehicles;
(d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the
information provided in the trip tickets prior to allowing payment thereof.

6.18. We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations and made additions
thereto and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) instruct the designated GSO to (a) evaluate the vehicles and heavy
equipment, especially those with a history of frequent breakdowns,
whether these are beyond economic repair and need replacement; and
(b) prepare the necessary reports to be submitted to the disposal
committee, if warranted, for the immediate disposal thereof; and (c)
maintain service history to monitor the repairs and replaced parts prior
the approval of purchase request;
(b) establish a motor pool, that will oversee the procurement of parts,
materials, and equipment necessary for vehicle maintenance and
repairs and the custody of each vehicle's records;
(c) appoint a permanent GSO that will oversee the operations of the to be
established motor pool;
(d) instruct the Municipal Accountant and the designated GSO to review
the deficiencies noted for future compliance prior to payment of
expenditures.

7.23. We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations and made supplemental
recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed in coordination with
the Municipal Accountant and Department Heads concerned to:
(e) reduce the amount spent on assorted groceries and serving meals/snacks
during ordinary staff meetings or conferences in order to prevent the
68
incurrence of unnecessary expenditures;
(f) adhere closely to the limitations supplied under Section 325 of the LGC on
reimbursement for discretionary purposes as exemplified above;
(g) pay all claims directly to the supplier and avert adopting reimbursement as
a mode for payment of expenses in consonance with Section 93 of P.D. 1445;
and
(h) instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (i) provide for the wide
dissemination of the Municipality’s procurement activities in order to
obtain/attract more suppliers by consolidating all future purchases
(planned events) for the year; and (ii) to employ competitive bidding as the
primary mode of procurement unless the conditions are present for the use
of most appropriate procurement modality such as shopping and small
value procurement.

8.31. We reiterated and made additional recommendations and the Local
Chief Executive agreed in coordination with the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer:
(a) convene the Municipal Development Council:
i. establish a formal procedure for Program/Project/Activity
(PPA) identification for inclusion in the 20% DF, Annual
Investment Program (AIP) that are specific, to be
implemented for the ensuing year to dispense the need to
secure such SB approval for the timely implementation of the
development projects, otherwise, seek approval from the SB
of projects charged to the appropriation described in generic
terms; and
ii. continuously refer to the JMC for appropriate guidance in
crafting the Annual Investment Program and inclusion of
PPAs on the 20% DF that will aim to fully attain the desired
socio-economic development targets of the Municipality, with
emphasis on the recent issuance on the utilization of the fund
and inclusion of budget intended for the implementation of
CBMS to aid in planning PPAs in the Municipality, anchored
with the LGUs long term and medium term development plan,
introduce amendments if warranted to match the ever-
evolving needs of the public.
(b) request the Sangguniang Bayan, as agents in setting the direction of
economic and social development, and coordinating development
efforts within its territorial jurisdiction and will promote the
general welfare of the locality and its inhabitants, to conduct
thorough review of the PPAs embodied in the proposed AIP that
are lucid and specific and are not contrary to law as enunciated
under the Local Government Code and the Budget Operations
Manual for LGUs, 2023 edition;
(c) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to take note of improper
charges noted in item 1.21 of this Memorandum to warrant the
strict utilization of the 20% Development Fund to only eligible
expenditures and, ultimately, capitalize on the Fund’s intended
purpose. Refrain from charging these items or provide allotment,
appropriately under the 20% Development Fund; and
(d) instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
i. submit the aforecited deficient documents identified in Item
1.24.4 and 1.24.5 for further evaluation by the Audit Team;
ii. monitor the proper titling of the lot purchased, to ensure
security and ownership to properties of the LGU; and
78
iii. check the noted deficiencies in item 1.24.6 above, in
conformance with the processes and documentary
requirements stated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012 to avert the recurrence thereof on future similar
undertakings and ensure the completeness of the documents
submitted prior to payment.

9.47. We reiterated and made supplemental audit recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive direct the:
(a) LDRRMO together with the LDRRMC, to (i) follow the PPAs
embodied in the 3-year plan and the LDRRMFIP, otherwise, the
proposed changes should be accompanied with adequate
recommendation and approval of the LDRRMC and Sangguniang
Bayan and LCE, respectively; (ii) decide on the use of unexpended
balance of the fund and incorporate the same in the LDRRMFIP; (iii)
in coordination with the MPDO see to it that all of the thematic areas
of the DRRM are sufficiently funded, consider funding the disaster
response, recovery and rehabilitation as well as augment the allotment
for the procurement of lifesaving, rescue equipment and the like; and
(iv) cause the gradual compliance with the minimum standards on
88
disaster preparedness based on available resources;
(b) Municipal Budget Officer to review noted inappropriate charges
identified in item 1.16. of the Memorandum and henceforth, avert
making charges not related to the planned PPAs to avoid possible
sanction such as technical malversation, for expenditures lacking
appropriation;
(c) Municipal Accountant along with the BAC and implementing offices
to (i) look into the noted common observations in item 1.20 of this
Report for consideration on future similar disbursements in
conformance with the processes and documentary requirements
stated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and DPWH DO
No. 163 dated October 23, 2015 prior to payment; and (ii) prepare
necessary entries to reflect the transfer of the unexpended balance of
the fund to the Special Trust Fund account; and
(d) Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the actual transfer of the
unexpended balance of the DRRMF to the trust fund account in
accordance with the above-cited Circular.

10.32. We restated our previous recommendation and made supplemental
recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:
(a) GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and the GAD Focal Point Person
(GFPP) as well as the Implementing Departments (i) strictly
implement the GAD PPAs in accordance with the DILG-approved
GAD plan and budget; (ii) amend the GPB to align PPAs to address
the current GAD issues, if warranted; and (iii) actively monitor the
effective implementation of the annual plan, introduce intervention
as necessary;
(b) in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development
Officer (MPDO), (i) work on the establishment of the GAD
database and maintenance thereof, in order to provide a reliable
baseline data for the establishment of PPAs for a more gender-
responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and (ii)
include in the GPB the institutionalization of the GAD database, as
deemed appropriate; and
(c) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the
Municipal Accountant (as internal auditor) to (i) strictly observe
the required allocation of the GAD fund in the approved budget;
(ii) charge the expenditures to their appropriate budget; and (iii)
refrain from charging ineligible expenditures to the fund.

11.44. We reiterated and supplemented our previous year’s recommendations
and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) require the BAC, BAC Secretariat, and BAC-TWG to comply strictly
with the procurement procedures ascribed under RA 9184, its IRR
and relevant rules and regulations in the Municipality’s procurement
process by:
i. in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to submit the
noted deficient documents of the projects as appearing in item
1.11.3, otherwise submit an explanation for non-submission
thereof, for further evaluation as basis for the Audit Team’s
107
subsequent audit action; preparation of an
explanation/justification on other deficiencies noted also
mentioned in Annex B, to avoid suspension of the transactions in
audit; and look into the noted deviations on the procurement of
goods and infrastructure projects, to avert the recurrence thereof
on future similar undertakings;
ii. in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, prepare and
submit Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as necessitated above to
ensure smooth execution of the procurement activities and avoid
delays in the implementation of projects;
iii. conduct pre-procurement conference on procurement of Goods
costing Two Million Pesos (₱2,000,000.00) and below,
procurement of Infrastructure Projects costing Five Million Pesos
(₱5,000,000.00) and below, and procurement of Consulting
Services costing One Million Pesos (₱1,000,000.00) and below;
iv. diligently observe timelines of procurement activities in
cognizance with the minimum and maximum possible time as
decreed under the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184;
v. adhere to the posting requirements of the procurement law,
particularly, on the posting of advertisements, Notices of Award
(NOA), Notices to Proceed (NTP), and Approved Contracts in
conspicuous places on the Local Government Unit (LGU)’s
premises and in the PhilGEPS website;
vi. furnishing COA with copies of all purchase orders/contracts and
all papers relative thereto within five days after the perfection of
the contract and sending of notification of the time and date of the
scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006;
vii. henceforth, on similar undertakings on infrastructure projects,
conduct complete detailed engineering prior to the preparation of
bid documents to assure the viability of infrastructure projects;
viii. on future undertakings, a) carefully scrutinize the documentary
requirements submitted by the bidder; and b) review thoroughly
all documentation and communications issued to avoid errors and
omissions; and
ix. attend to procurement trainings relative to the New Government
Procurement Law to keep abreast with the latest requirements
thereof.
(b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify all supporting documents
as to completeness and ensure that the same formed part of the
documents supporting the disbursement before effecting payments
thereof.

12.17. We reiterated our prior years audit recommendations and supplement
additions thereto and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) observe the pro-forma entries for Trust
Fund provided under the MNGAS for LGUs for future transactions;
(ii) evaluate the noted unrecorded assets in the GF and cause
necessary correcting entries as warranted;
(b) the Municipal Accountant and implementing departments to (i)
henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of the MOA and guidelines
in the implementation of programs/projects such as
equity/counterpart requirements, among others; (ii) henceforth, only
award the grants to the beneficiaries included in the approved list or
project proposal, and seek for approval to the Source Agencies before
108
effecting changes in the program/project; (iii) submit a clarification
on noted departures from the utilization guidelines of the fund
transfers; (iv) submit the aforecited deficient documents identified in
item 1.13 of this observation; and (v) henceforth, ensure that the
expenditures/project charged against to the funds received were in
accordance with the intent of the SA to avoid disallowance in audit;
and
(c) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan to revise the Municipal
Ordinance for the proper disposition of the PhilHealth capitation
payment from Konsulta+ .

13.23. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that:
(a) except for cash advances for training and travel, all special disbursing
officers are formally designated and adequately bonded prior to the grant
of the initial cash advance;
112
(b) the Municipal Accountant (i) checks the completeness of the supporting
documents, and signatories on liquidation reports; (ii) issues certification
that the previous cash advance is liquidated/accounted for before the grant
of another cash advance; (iii) lecturers/resource persons are paid in
accordance with Budget Circular 2007-1; and
(c) granting of CAs is limited to disbursements allowed by law and adheres
closely to the requirements of the procurement law or RA 9184 and resorts
to cash advance only in meritorious cases.

14.16. We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and supplemented
additional corrective measures and the Local Chief Executive agreed to
instruct the:
(a) Municipal Accountant and General Services Office to (a) provide the
IC the available data and property records needed in the conduct of
physical inventory of PPEs and inventories (for building contents);
(b) Inventory Committee to (i) conduct Annual Physical Inventory of the
LGU’s property to ensure the existence, accuracy and completeness of
insurable properties of the Municipality; (ii) coordinate with the Local
Appraisal Committee for the determination of assessed value (as basis
of the premium payment); and facilitate the preparation and
submission of the required PIF with the GSIS for the uninsured and
yearly renewal of properties;
(c) Municipal Accountant to (i) henceforth see to it that the declared
values in the PIF were duly supported with appraisal report of Local
Appraisal Committee for properties with value of ₱10,000,000.00 and
below. Otherwise, hire an independent appraiser as needed; and (ii)
check the completeness of documents supporting payment of
premiums before making payments; and
(d) Budget Officer to continue setting aside funds for the payment of the
premiums of the insurable properties owned by the LGU.

15.24. We reiterated and made additional recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the
BPLO, to:
(a) obtain adequate training in the examination of books of accounts to be
able to conduct the same and ascertain that proper amounts of taxes were
assessed and collected;
(b) conduct re-assessment/evaluation of assessed business tax on remaining
items not tested in CY 2024 and consider collecting deficiencies, if any.
This action was proposed since the conducted random sampling resulted
and divulged several deviations;
(c) review the formulas and tax tables encoded in the system and ensure that these are i

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require the: (a)	BAC, BAC Secretariat, and BAC-TWG to (i) relentlessly improve the quality and extent of their compliance with the requirements of RA 9184, its IRR, and relevant rules and regulations in the Municipality’s procurement process by paying attention to the procurement lapses noted above, to avoid the recurrence thereof in future procurements; (ii) enforce the required payment of bidding documents before the deadline of submission of bids; (iii) secure a copy of Certification from the Insurance Commission of the surety bonds posted by the suppliers/contractors by sending confirmation letters to the issuing insurance company which they will forward to the Insurance Commission; (iv) observe the threshold set by the IRR on the alternative modes of procurement; (b)	BAC, Accounting and other concerned officials to submit the lacking documents for further evaluation which will be the basis for the Audit Team’s subsequent audit action; and (c)	Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the required supporting documents for every transaction before any payment is made.

We also recommended that the LCE consider creating a separate office for the BAC Secretariat or hiring additional personnel assigned to assist in the review of bid documents to minimize errors and improve compliance with RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management with its Bids and Awards Committee will comply with the procurement process and will complete the necessary documents required by the Procurement Law.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive: (a)	consider the disposal of the vehicles that were identified as beyond economic repair and use the proceeds from the disposal thru a supplemental budget as additional funds for the purchase of new vehicles;  (b)	direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a maintenance/repair record of each piece of equipment/vehicle owned and operated by the LGU to aid in the monitoring, if vehicles meet the conditions of “beyond economic repair” for appropriate disposition; and (c)	instruct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to review the deficiencies noted for future compliance prior to payment of expenditures. 

We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive appoint a permanent General Services Officers based on merit and fitness, who will take charge in the monitoring of supplies and properties such as monitoring of excessive repairs of vehicles as mentioned above.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has agreed with the recommendations.
The Management through its Inventory Appraisal Committee will prepare the request for disposal for the unserviceable vehicles and will procure additional vehicles as included in the APP.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive: (a)	instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to adopt public bidding as the primary mode of procurement for grocery items unless the conditions are present for the use of most appropriate procurement modality such as shopping and small value procurement; (b)	adhere strictly to the provision that payments should be made directly to the suppliers and limit adopting reimbursement as a mode of payment in consonance with Section 93 of P.D. 1445; and (c)	observe economic measures in the utilization of government funds. Rather, channel the resources to the implementation of projects and programs that are more beneficial to the constituents of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management though its GSO and BAC will comply to the provisions of the Procurement Law and will limit the reimbursement of the assorted groceries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) implement the timely preparation and submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statement for LandBank Account under General Fund for immediate detection of errors and correction thereof; and (ii) look into the noted reconciling items and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary to correct and establish the reliability of the Cash in Bank balance presented in the financial statement; and
direct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) facilitate the enrollment of LandBank WeAccess to ease the tedious work of traditional banking. Enrollment in e-banking may help the easier access of its account that can address the abovementioned difficulty of obtaining bank statements on time; and (ii) facilitate the transmittal thereof to the Municipal Accountant for BRS preparation.

We reiterated our prior years recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
require the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all purchases of supplies and materials are recorded under the proper inventory account; (ii) prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as called for in the above-quoted regulation; and (iii) ensure complete documentation in reference to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and (iv) examine noted deviations in item 2.22, for future similar transactions and adhere closely with the requirements of the law, preceding to payment thereof; and
instruct the Designated-GSO to (i) prepare and maintain stock cards for the purchased supplies; (ii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; (iii) procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed; and (iv) request a designated area to store the procured supplies to facilitate the proper monitoring of stocks and usage thus, spoilage can be prevented.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
instruct the concerned officials to (i) review the requirements of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPEs, in order to have verifiable data of PPEs as to the existence, condition, and accountability to be able to subsequently comply with the requirements on the conduct of annual inventory, preparation and maintenance of records (Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards); and (ii) renew PAR within the prescribed period;
along with concerned officials, cause the observance of the procedures necessary for the reporting and disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU starting with the preparation and submission of IIRUP; and
require the designated General Services Office Head and the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to prepare the Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
consider the disposal of the vehicles that were identified as beyond economic repair and use the proceeds from the disposal thru a supplemental budget as additional funds for the purchase of new vehicles;
direct the designated GSO to maintain a maintenance/repair record of each piece of equipment/vehicle owned and operated by the LGU to aid in the monitoring, if vehicles meet the conditions of “beyond economic repair” for appropriate disposition; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to review the deficiencies noted for future compliance prior to payment of expenditures.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to appoint a permanent General Services Officers based on merit and fitness, who will take charge in the monitoring of supplies and properties such as monitoring of excessive repairs of vehicles as mentioned above.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive and Department Heads concerned agreed to:
reduce the amount spent on assorted groceries and serving meals/snacks during ordinary staff meetings or conferences in order to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures;
pay all claims directly to the supplier and avert adopting reimbursement as a mode for payment of expenses in consonance with Section 93 of P.D. 1445; and
provide for the wide dissemination of the Municipality’s procurement activities in order to obtain/attract more suppliers by consolidating all future purchases (planned events) for the year and instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to employ competitive bidding as the primary mode of procurement unless the conditions are present for the use of most appropriate procurement modality such as shopping and small value procurement.

We reiterated and made supplemental audit recommendations thereof and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
LDRRMO together with the LDRRMC, to (i) prepare and submit annually the LDRRMFIP in the required format in reference to COA Circular 2012-002, inclusive of the plan relative to the unexpended balance of the fund for the current year and the trust fund for DRRM; (ii) follow the PPAs embodied in the 3-year plan, otherwise, the proposed changes should be accompanied with adequate recommendation and approval of the LDRRMC and Sangguniang Bayan and LCE, respectively; (iii) to reduce the amount allotted for labor payroll on waterways and consider proposing a balanced budget for its LDRRMF to address all the four thematic areas particularly on the purchase of necessary lifesaving and rescue equipment and similar items; and (iv) cause the gradual compliance with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources;
Municipal Accountant along with the implementing offices to (i) look into the noted common observations in item 8.14 of this Report for consideration on future similar disbursements in conformance with the processes and documentary requirements stated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and DPWH DO No. 163 dated October 23, 2015 prior to payment; (ii) prepare necessary entries to reflect the transfer of the unexpended balance of the fund to the Special Trust Fund account; and
Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the actual transfer of the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to the trust fund account in accordance with the above-cited Circular.

We restated our previous recommendation and made supplemental recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the GAD Focal Point Person (GFPP):
in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), work on the establishment of a GAD database and the maintenance thereof that will provide a reliable baseline data for the establishment of PPAs for a more gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
continue with the close monitoring of the implementation of the programs and projects embodied in the approved GPB by the DILG to ensure full attainment of GAD targets/objectives, intervene immediately if delays in the implementation are observed and amend the GPB, if warranted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct:
the Municipal Accountant and implementing departments to (i) henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of the MOA and guidelines in the implementation of programs/projects such as equity/counterpart requirements, among others; and (ii) secure a copy of either MOA or guidelines to ensure that the expenditures charged against thereto were in accordance with the intent of the SA to avoid disallowance in audit;
the concerned department/s to submit the aforecited deficient documents identified in Item 11.18. for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and
the Municipal Accountant to (i) observe the pro-forma entries for Trust Fund provided under the MNGAS for LGUs for future transactions; and (ii) evaluate the noted unrecorded PPEs in the GF and cause necessary correcting entries as warranted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the BPLO, to:
obtain adequate training in the examination of books of accounts to be able to conduct the same and ascertain that proper amounts of taxes were assessed and collected;
establish a stratified schedule to set a certain income level standard for various business entities after careful consideration of various industry factors as the basis for a presumptive income level to be embodied in the Municipality’s duly approved local tax ordinance; and
coordinate with DICT for the identified technical glitch in the web-based system and constantly monitor data encoded therein to facilitate appropriate and timely communication of the recurrence of similar errors.

We reiterated our prior recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the:
Inventory Committee to conduct Annual Physical Inventory of the LGU’s property and furnish the results thereof to the General Services Officer (GSO);
Budget Officer to continue setting aside funds for the payment of the premiums of the insurable properties owned by the LGU; and
On uninsured properties, GSO to (i) coordinate with the GSIS for the required documents on the application and assessment of insurance and premium, respectively; (ii) coordinate with the Inventory Committee and the Appraisal Committee the lacking data for the immediate preparation and submission of the required PIF with the GSIS; and (iii) facilitate payment of premium due to the GIF relative to insurable properties.

We reiterated and made additional recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
instruct the HRMO (i) to continuously look for possible candidates from the array of job order employees for permanency based on available vacant positions for recommendation to the Personnel Selection Board (PSB) to reduce the number of job orders in the Municipality, taking into consideration the mandated PS limitation under the Local Government Code; (ii) evaluate/determine the necessity of the hired job orders in the LGU by objectively conducting work evaluation or devise a performance indicator system/mechanism which will identify the performing/non-performing personnel and as basis for the salary increase, along with immediate superiors of concerned JOs, in order to assess the actual need for subject personnel and ultimately reduce the amount of expenses related thereto; (iii) in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, review the LGU’s organizational structure and staffing pattern in consideration with the service requirement as well as financial capability of the LGU and recommend for the creation of additional plantilla positions, if warranted; and (iv)
direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Officer to look into the noted deficiencies in Item 14.19 of this Report and ensure compliance or effect corrections needed, before allowing payments thereof.

We restated our prior recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
continue with their compliance with the proper remittance of the withheld amounts for GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; and
maintain a complete Index of Payments for proper monitoring of claims and for prompt and accurate recording and remittances to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG.

We reiterated our prior years recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:

(a)	consider the hiring of a permanent GSO who will process all purchasing concerns and keep track of the movement of stocks on hand from receipt until issuance for accounting, control and accountability purposes; 

(b)	instruct the Designated-GSO to (i) prepare and maintain stock cards for the purchased supplies; (ii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and (iii) procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed; and

(c)	require the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all purchases of supplies and materials are recorded under the proper inventory account; (ii) prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as called for in the above-quoted regulation; and (iii) examine noted deviations, for future similar transactions and adhere closely with the requirements of the law, preceding to payment thereof.
observations.managementAction:
The management has agreed with the recommendations. With the new designation of the GSO, the recommendations will be properly addressed. Additional personnel will be requested by the Accounting Office to accomplish the required documentation and procedure for handling inventories for supplies. The management has already considered purchasing common-used supplies to PS-DBM, but due to limited supplies from PS-DBM the Management resorted to purchasing from other local suppliers.
The distribution list required from the disbursement identified in the Annex will be provided to the COA team.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive: (a)	instruct the concerned officials to (i) review the requirements of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPEs, in order to have verifiable data of PPEs as to the existence, condition and accountability to be able to subsequently comply with the requirements on the conduct of annual inventory, preparation and maintenance of records (Property Ledger Cards and Stock Cards); and (ii) renew PAR within the prescribed period; (b)	instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the noted transactions attached herein and prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries, if warranted, to correct the misstatements of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that costs of major repairs, rehabilitation, improvement, and construction of infrastructure assets should be capitalized or recognized as property, plant and equipment, in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 17 and related rules on asset recognition as cited above;  (c) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to adequately appropriate funds classified under Capital Outlays instead of allotments for MOOE in the LGU’s Annual Budget for transactions that are capital in nature such as construction and major repairs of buildings and infrastructures to facilitate the correct accounting recognition as required by Section 50 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and (d)require the designated General Services Office Head and the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to prepare the Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered.

We also recommended that the LCE, along with concerned officials, cause the observance of the procedures necessary for the reporting and disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU starting with the preparation and submission of IIRUP.
observations.managementAction:
With the designation of the new GSO and the Inventory and Appraisal Committee, the recommendations will be properly addressed.
With the new personnel in Accounting Office, the required documents and procedures will be properly addressed as well.

Upon the completion of the physical count, the Inventory and Appraisal Committee will recommend the unserviceable properties to be disposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive: (a) henceforth, instruct the implementing departments to (i) strictly observed the provisions of the MOA and issued guidelines in the implementation of programs/projects; and (ii) communicate accordingly, changes that will be made to the program/project with the Source Agency and seek the approval thereof prior to implementation. (b)	instruct the concerned department/s to submit the aforecited deficient documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Management has agreed with the recommendations and will strictly comply with the provisions stated in the MOAs of fund transfers.

Gasoline was provided to motor vehicles not owned by the LGU as means of assistance to augment the gasoline expense needed in the operations of the National Government Agencies particularly the PNP, BJMP, BFP, DILG in which their assistance is beneficial in maintaining the peace and order of the LGU.
Henceforth, the Management will accomplish the required documentation in the issuance of gasoline and will limit the issuance of gasoline to vehicles not owned by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive and Department Heads concerned to: (a)	reduce the amount spent on serving meals and snacks during ordinary staff meetings or conferences in order to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures;  (b)	pay all claims directly to the supplier and avert adopting reimbursement as a mode for payment of expenses in consonance with Section 93 of P.D. 1445; (c)	consolidate all future purchases (planned events) for the year and employ competitive bidding for meals and snacks served to be assured that advantageous prices for the LGU are obtained; and (d)	provide for the wide dissemination of the Municipality’s procurement activities in order to obtain/attract more suppliers.
observations.managementAction:
Management will limit disbursements for meals and snacks and observe prudence on expense for food and entertainment for guests and meetings.

Disbursements were made with the required documents such as attendance, pictures and programs.
Budget for these expenses was strictly followed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated and made additional recommendations that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer thru the Local Development Council, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant: (a)  continue the checking/regular monitoring of the fund’s utilization in harmony with the approved AIP; (b) reduce/limit the amounts earmarked for expenditures that do not partake the nature of capital investments such as labor payroll for solid waste management and clearing/removal of debris and, if budget permits, provide allotment in the General Fund proper for such expenses, if occurrence thereof is unavoidable; (c)	present in detail the PPAs charged against the 20% DF in the Annual Investment Program (AIP); and (d)	review the noted deviations to warrant the strict utilization of the 20% Development Fund to only eligible expenditures and, ultimately, maximize the Fund’s intended purpose.
observations.managementAction:
The Management through its Local Development council will properly plan and specifically identify the priority development projects in the AIP.
Proper monitoring on the utilization of fund will be done and will strictly comply with the necessary guidelines in the utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated and made additions to our prior year’s audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE): (a)	instruct the LDRRMO, together with the LDRRMC, (i) to include the unexpended balance of the fund in the LDRRMFIP for proper planning and utilization of the Fund; and (ii) cause the gradual compliance with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; and (b)	instruct the Municipal Accountant to cause the recognition of the unrecorded Property, Plant and Equipment, and observe the proper recording of the amounts as provided by the Circular.
observations.managementAction:
The LDRRMC will convene and judiciously plan on the unexpended balances on what PPA they will implement according to disaster thematic areas and to the priority of the municipality.

The Municipal Accountant will record the unrecorded transfer into the necessary accounts in the books.

observations.recommendation: We restated our previous recommendation and made supplemental recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require the: (a)	GAD Focal Point Person (GFPP) to (i) amend the GPB to match the pre-prepared GPB with the approved appropriation in the annual budget for the budget year, subject to the review and approval of the DILG; (ii) closely monitor the implementation of the programs and projects embodied in the GPB and intervene immediately if delays in the implementation are observed and amend the GPB, if warranted; and (iii) in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), work on the establishment of a GAD database and the maintenance thereof for a more gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation specifically on attending the required technical orientation on the usage of the CBMS; and (b)	instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the requirements on the preparation of Subsidiary Ledgers in consonance with COA Circular 2021-008.
observations.managementAction: The Management through its GAD Council will comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and made additional recommendations that the Local Chief Executive: (a)	instruct the HRMO (i) to continuously look for possible candidates from the array of job order employees for permanency based on available vacant positions for recommendation to the Personnel Selection Board (PSB) to reduce the number of job orders in the Municipality, taking into consideration the mandated PS limitation under the Local Government Code; (ii) evaluate the necessity of the hired job orders in order to reduce the amount of expenses; and (iii) in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, review the LGU’s organizational structure and staffing pattern in consideration with the service requirement as well as financial capability of the LGU and recommend for the creation of additional plantilla positions, if warranted. (b)	direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Officer to look into the noted deficiencies and ensure compliance or effect corrections needed, before allowing payments thereof.
observations.managementAction:
The Management agrees with the recommendations.
For the year 2023, as of this date, there were already 13 permanent positions offered to qualified job order personnel. And the Management will continue to hire qualified personnel for permanency on the available vacant positions.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive: (a)	continue to coordinate efforts with the barangay officials on the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003;  (b)	seek the help of private companies/organizations and schools in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste; (c)	formulate solid waste management programs that would encourage and motivate the public to give their cooperation and full support; and (d)	exercise the strong political will to implement the applicable ordinances pertaining to Solid Waste Management, particularly on the implementation of the “No littering” ordinance ascribed under Municipal Ordinance No. 012 series of 2018.
observations.managementAction: The Management has agreed with the recommendations and will exert best efforts in the full implementation of RA 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the: (a)	Local School Board to (i) include in the succeeding years work financial plan the use the unexpended balance of the SEF to supplement the operations and maintenance of schools in adherence to existing guidelines for the utilization of the SEF; and (b)	Local Finance Committee to, henceforth, inform the LSB of any surplus arising from savings and excess SET collections to fully address the needs of the schools in the locality.
observations.managementAction:
The past administration has only approved a specific amount of budget for the SEF without considering the excess collections received in SEF resulting to the accumulation of savings from the past years. However, the past administration has substantially addressed the needs of the school with its SEF Budget.

The Management through its Local School Board will consider the recommendations and will specifically identify priority projects for the utilization of the accrued surplus in SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the: (a)	Inventory Committee to conduct Annual Physical Inventory of the LGU’s property and furnish the results thereof to the General Services Officer (GSO); (b)	Appraisal Committee to determine the value of the LGU’s insurable properties for the determination of the correct insurance premiums due to the GIF; (c)	Finance Committee to appropriately set aside funds for the payment of the premiums of the insurable properties owned by the LGU; and (d)	GSO to (i) coordinate with the GSIS for the required documents on the application and assessment of insurance and premium, respectively; (ii)	coordinate with the Inventory Committee and the Appraisal Committee the lacking data for the immediate preparation and submission of the required PIF with the GSIS; and (iii)	facilitate payment of premium due to the GIF relative to insurable properties.
observations.managementAction:
The Management will facilitate the insurance of the Municipal Properties through its GSO and the Inventory and Appraisal Committee in compliance with Property Insurance Law and COA Circular 2018-002.
Proper monitoring will be done for the timely renewal for the insurance of the said properties. Sufficient funds will be appropriated and allotted for the insurance of said properties thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the: (a)	Municipal Planning Officer to (i) coordinate with the DENR-PENRO for the renewal of the Memorandum of Agreement that will serve as a basis for the payment of rental/user’s fees pending the approval of the SAPA specifically on terms regarding share on tickets sold; (ii) submit justification with other necessary documents to support the waived user’s fee for the period discussed earlier to lessen the amount payable to the DENR; and (iii) settle the accrued user’s fees as determined by the DENR. (b)	Municipal Finance Committee to (i) conduct an analysis on the viability of the Mt. Arayat Natural Park’s operation; and (ii) communicate the results thereof to the LCE for decision-making in the continuity of operation of the said park.
observations.managementAction: The Management agrees with the recommendation. The unrecorded amount of ₱937,580.41 will be recorded in the books and settlement of due amount will be made.

(b)	provide the Accounting Office with additional personnel, if possible, to aid in the preparation of the Index of Payment and immediate detection of errors in remittances.
observations.managementAction: The Management has agreed with the recommendations and will hire the additional personnel in the Accounting Office to accomplish the required documentary requirements and proper monitoring of the employees’ contributions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) look into the cause of the variances in the remittance of these amounts to BIR; and (b) henceforth, ascertain that taxes withheld are remitted intact.
observations.managementAction:
The Management particularly the accounting office strictly adheres to compliance to tax laws. Subsidiary ledgers were already maintained. The variance noted were already reconciled. 
Moving forward, the accounting office will regularly reconcile any variance.

observations.managementAction: The Management has agreed to comply with the recommendations and apply the requirements of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPEs.

observations.managementAction: The Management will stop the issuance of fuel to private vehicles and comply with the recommendations

observations.recommendation:

members of the BAC provide for a wide dissemination of the Municipality’s procurement activities in order to obtain/attract more suppliers;  

top management (i) observe economy and austerity on expenses for food and entertainment of guests and on catering services during conferences and consultative meetings among constituents and

Municipal Accountant, as the Internal Auditor of the LGU, check that supporting documents such as attendance sheets and the dates and venues of meetings are properly accomplished and indicated for proper evaluation of documents.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has procured meals and snacks to limited suppliers because these are most available when needed which can accommodate the requirements on urgent basis, as the Management only procure meals as the need arises. 
Some disbursements were accomplished with the required documents such as attendance, pictures and programs.

The Management will limit the disbursements for meals and snacks and observe prudence on expenses for food and entertainment for guests and meetings.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
consider the hiring of a permanent GSO who will process all purchasing concerns and keep track of the movement of stocks on hand from receipt until issuance for accounting, control and accountability purposes;
instruct the Designated-GSO to (i) prepare and maintain stock cards for the purchased supplies; (ii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and (iii) procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed; and
require the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all purchases of supplies and materials are recorded under the proper inventory account; (ii) prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as called for in the above-quoted regulation; and (iii) examine noted deviations in item 1.24. of this memorandum, for future similar transactions, adhere closely with the requirements of the law, preceding to payment thereof.

We reiterated and the Management agreed with our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to:
review the requirements of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPEs, in order to have a verifiable data of PPEs as to existence, condition and accountability to be able to subsequently comply with the requirements on the conduct of annual inventory, preparation and maintenance of records (Property Ledger Cards and Stock Cards); and
renew PAR with an emphasis on the creation of Inventory Committee.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) review the noted transactions attached herein and prepare the appropriate adjusting journal entries, if warranted, to correct the misstatements of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that costs of major repairs, rehabilitation, improvement, and construction of infrastructure assets are capitalized or recognized as property, plant and equipment and likewise, recognize the unrecorded assets in the General Fund from the Trust Fund account, in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 17 and related rules on asset recognition as cited above; and (ii) exert effort in the gathering of data to determine the appropriate useful life and residual value of the cited undepreciated PPEs for the immediate recognition of depreciation thereof;
direct the Municipal Budget Officer to adequately appropriate funds classified under Capital Outlays instead of allotments for MOOE in the LGU’s Annual Budget for transactions that are capital in nature such as construction and major repairs of buildings and infrastructures to facilitate the correct accounting recognition as required by Section 50 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
if possible, appoint permanent General Services Officer and increase the support staff of the GSO to carry out regular functions, particularly the conduct of annual physical count and maintenance of property records; and
require the designated General Services Office Head and the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to prepare the Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered.

We reiterated and the management agreed with our previous year’s audit recommendations and made supplemental recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require the:
BAC, BAC Secretariat and BAC-TWG to (i) relentlessly improve the quality and extent of their compliance with the requirements of RA 9184, its IRR and relevant rules and regulations in the Municipality’s procurement process by paying attention on the procurement lapses noted above, to avoid the recurrence thereof in future procurements; (ii) properly plan their procurement activities through the preparation and consolidation of Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMPs) to be used as basis in the preparation of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and which contains all the anticipated materials, services, goods, civil works requirements and the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the same into an Annual Procurement Plan within the prescribed period; (iii) enforce the required payment of bidding documents before the deadline of submission of bids; (iv) secure a copy of Certification from the Insurance Commission of the surety bonds posted by the suppliers/contractors by sending confirmation letters to the issuing insurance company which they will forward to the Insurance Commission; (v) observe the threshold set by the IRR on the alternative modes of procurement; (vi) avoid/stop splitting of contracts in compliance with the revised IRR of RA 9184.
end-users or implementing units in the next budgeting period to prepare Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) based on the format prescribed by the Government Policy Procurement Board (GPPB) as basis of the budget for the ensuing year;
BAC, Accounting and other concerned officials to submit the lacking documents of the projects mentioned in Annex E, for further evaluation as basis for the Audit Team’s subsequent audit action; and
Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the required supporting documents for every transaction before any payment is made.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the implementing departments to see to it that reportorial requirements as well as the timelines of implementation are strictly observed and that all disbursements charged against funds, held in trust, are in accordance with their intended purpose set forth by the guidelines and/or MOA.
instruct the concerned departments to submit the aforecited deficient documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
adhere closely with the aforementioned rules and regulations and assess the urgency and necessity of travels prior to approval; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) adhere to the allowable travel rates prescribed under EO 77 and establish the actual fare at the prevailing rates; and (ii) ensure proper and complete documentary requirements, including proper approving authority of Travel Orders, before allowing payment thereof to ensure the propriety and validity of claims.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
require the refund from the personnel concerned of the amounts corresponding to the value of gasoline issued for use of non-government vehicles; and
stop the practice of providing gasoline/fuel to privately-owned vehicles against Municipal funds pursuant to Section 340(e) Volume I of GAAM.
We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
require the concerned drivers to prepare the trip ticket judiciously prior to trips leading to the completion of data needed in the trip tickets; and
Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the information provided in the trip ticket prior to allowing payment thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
adopt public bidding as the primary mode of procurement for grocery items, otherwise, ensure that the most appropriate procurement modality is implemented by officials responsible for the procurement;
adhere strictly to the provision that payments should be made directly to the suppliers and limit adopting reimbursement as a mode of payment in consonance with Section 93 of PD 1445; and
observe economy measures in the utilization of government funds. Preferably, channel the resources to the implementation of projects and programs that are more beneficial to the constituents of the Municipality.

We restated and made additional recommendation and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer thru the Local Development Council, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant:
reduce/limit the amounts earmarked for expenditures that do not partake the nature of capital investments such as labor payroll for solid waste management and desilting/declogging of canals as such can be charged to the General Fund proper;
present the PPAs in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) charged under the 20% DF in detail; and
review the noted deviations under item 8.16., to warrant the strict utilization of the 20% Development Fund to only eligible expenditures, and ultimately to maximize the Fund’s intended purpose.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and further recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the HRMO (i) to continuously look for possible candidates from the array of job order employees for permanency based on available vacant positions for recommendation to the Personnel Selection Board (PSB) of the Municipality to reduce the number of job orders in the Municipality, taking into consideration the mandated PS limitation under the Local Government Code; (ii) finalize the drafted policy guidelines, to address the issues on the necessity and propriety of hiring job orders and rates allowable pursuant to Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules on appointments and Other Personnel Actions; and (iii) evaluate the necessity of the hired job orders in order to reduce the amount of expenses coupled therein; and
direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Officer to look into the noted deficiencies and ensure the compliance or effect corrections needed, before allowing payments thereof.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
coordinate efforts with the barangay officials on the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003;
seek the help of private companies/organizations and schools in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste; and
formulate solid waste management programs that would encourage and motivate the public to give their cooperation and full support.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the immediate promulgation of the “No Segregation, No Collection” ordinance to institutionalize the SWM system of the Municipality; and
exercise strong political will to implement the applicable ordinances pertaining to Solid Waste Management particularly on the implementation of the “No littering” ordinance as ascribed under Municipal Ordinance No. 012 series of 2018.

We reiterated our last year’s audit recommendation that the officials concerned be more prudent and consider the repair of only those vehicles which are not beyond economic repair to save on incurring unnecessary costs that may be utilized for other priority programs for the benefit of constituents.

 Local Chief Executive (i) minimize or stop the practice of serving meals and snacks during ordinary staff meetings or conferences in order to prevent the incurrence of irregular and unnecessary expenditures and wastage of government funds;

A feasibility study and survey should be conducted prior to the preparation and approval of the development plan.  The process of public consultation is required to gain public support for the identified projects and would ensure that public services being given by the local government are responsive to the needs and wants of the constituents.

We recommended that the (a) members of the BAC and Secretariat provide for a wide dissemination of the Municipality’s procurement activities in order to obtain/attract more suppliers;  (b) top management (i) observe economy and prudence on expenses for food and entertainment of guests and on  catering services during conferences and consultative meetings among constituents and (ii) adequately plan events to provide sufficient time for the conduct of public bidding/eligibility checking; and (c) Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents such as   attendance sheets and the dates and venues of meetings are properly accomplished and indicated for proper evaluation of documents.

No. 9003;  (b) request the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof;  (c) enjoin the Sanggunian Bayan to pass an ordinance on the “no segregation, no collection” policy; and (d) initiate intensive information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with the DepEd officials in the Municipality and barangay officials.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management (a) require the LDRRMC to strictly formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP pursuant to the provisions of RA 10121, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and COA Circular 2012-002; and (b) require the Accounting Unit to prepare the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis which shall be submitted through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.
We also recommended that the Municipality stop the utilization of the calamity fund for activities other than those specified under RA 10121.

We reiterated our last year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct (a) all officials concerned involved in the procurement of the goods for calamity to adhere to the laws, rules and regulations  on procurement including the proper documentation of the transaction in all stages from initiation to payment thereof; and (b) the Municipal Accountant as the Internal Auditor of the LGU,  to see to it that all transactions are fully documented and all information called for are correctly supplied on each document or form.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to submit the required documents as discussed above, for further evaluation and subsequent audit action.

We recommended that management:
require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documents and that all  data and information needed are supplied including proper approvals before any payment is made;
submit the required documents stated above and strictly monitor compliance with the documentary requirements on future disbursements and payments; and
submit the legal basis for the payment of ₱12,000.00 Bayanihan Fund and henceforth, identify the specific provision of law, rule or regulation covering disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point Person and its TWG
comply strictly with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and ensure that the expenses incurred for GAD are only those responsive to GAD issues and concerns.
ensure that duly approved programs, projects and activities as stated in the approved annual GAD Plan  be implemented accordingly.

We recommended and the Members of the Local School Board agreed to:
prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization in reference to the abovementioned rules and regulations and the latest guidelines under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 S 2017;
maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprograming, if necessary; and
submit to the Audit Team the required documentations as enumerated above for evaluation and subsequent audit action and henceforth, comply with the required documentation for similar transactions in the future.
